name: Svelte
description: A compiler-based UI framework that shifts work from runtime to build time for minimal bundle sizes

description.value: A compiler-based UI framework that shifts work from runtime to build time for minimal bundle sizes

active-maintenance.comment: Very active with 825 contributors and frequent releases

reactivity-model.comment: Svelte 5 uses runes ($state, $derived, $effect) - a signals-based system compiled at build time

component-model.comment: Single-file components with .svelte extension combining HTML, CSS, and JS/TS

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative templates compiled to efficient imperative code

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in reactivity via runes ($state, $derived) and stores

opinionatedness.comment: Provides conventions and built-in features while remaining flexible

backward-compatibility.comment: Svelte 5 introduced breaking changes with runes; migration guides provided

primary-language.comment: Supports both JavaScript and TypeScript natively

typescript-support.comment: Native TypeScript support with full type inference in templates

template-syntax.comment: Single-file components with HTML-like template syntax and {#if}, {#each} blocks

styling-approach.comment: CSS in <style> tags is automatically scoped to components

build-required.comment: Compiler-based approach requires a build step

computed-values.comment: $derived rune for computed values that auto-update

two-way-binding.comment: bind: directive for two-way data binding

event-handling.comment: on: directive and onclick handlers for event binding

component-slots.comment: <slot> element for content projection with named slots

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in portal; typically handled via SvelteKit or third-party libraries

suspense.comment: {#await} blocks for async loading with pending/then/catch

error-boundaries.comment: No built-in error boundaries; handled via onError lifecycle or SvelteKit

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: bind:this for direct DOM element access

official-router.comment: SvelteKit provides routing; no standalone first-party router

router-name.comment: SvelteKit is the official meta-framework with file-based routing

route-guards.comment: Via SvelteKit's load functions and hooks

built-in-global-state.comment: Svelte stores (writable, readable, derived) for global state

global-state-library.comment: Built-in stores with writable, readable, and derived APIs

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Svelte DevTools browser extension for state inspection

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via community libraries

official-cli.comment: sv CLI (formerly create-svelte) for project scaffolding

hot-module-replacement.comment: HMR via Vite integration; preserves state during development

browser-devtools.comment: Svelte DevTools browser extension for Chrome and Firefox

vs-code-extension.comment: Official Svelte for VS Code extension with IntelliSense and formatting

error-messages.comment: Excellent compiler errors with precise locations and helpful suggestions

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent interactive tutorial, comprehensive docs, clear migration guides

bundle-size-core.comment: ~3-4 kB for the signals runtime; compiled components have minimal overhead

bundle-size-full.comment: ~10 kB typical for simple apps; much smaller than runtime-based frameworks

virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; compiles to direct DOM manipulation

compile-time-optimization.comment: Core to Svelte's design; moves reactivity work to compile time

lazy-components.comment: Dynamic imports and SvelteKit's lazy loading

partial-hydration.comment: Full hydration; partial hydration available via Astro integration

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Svelte 5 performs excellently in benchmarks; exact geometric mean varies by version

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~2.1 million weekly downloads as of December 2025

component-libraries.comment: Skeleton, shadcn-svelte, Flowbite Svelte, Carbon Components Svelte, Smelte, daisyUI

meta-framework.comment: Official meta-framework from the Svelte team

major-companies-using.value: Apple, Spotify, The New York Times, Square, Ikea, Brave, Bloomberg, Reuters

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Rich Harris (creator) hired by Vercel; strong community funding via Open Collective

job-market-demand.comment: Growing demand but still niche compared to React/Vue; highly admired framework

testing-library-name.comment: Recommended setup with Vitest as test runner

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress; Playwright recommended

pwa-support.comment: Via SvelteKit service worker support and Vite PWA plugin

learning-curve.comment: Very gentle learning curve; leverages existing HTML/CSS/JS knowledge

migration-path.comment: Comprehensive migration guide from Svelte 4 to 5

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be added to existing apps; compiles to vanilla JS
